danger_signals.visual_form: incoming fire patterns from mechanical fortress and enemy ships
danger_signals.meaning: immediate threat requiring player evasion or defense

danger_signals.visual_form: large mechanical orbiting structure with distinct architectural sections
danger_signals.meaning: primary world threat — mysterious mechanical fortress with plans to take control of Earth

danger_signals.visual_form: horizontally scrolling environment revealing new enemy formations
danger_signals.meaning: escalating danger through six sequential stages toward fortress confrontation

safety_signals.visual_form: collectible items representing one of seven weapon types (Striker, Reverse shot, Six Way Shot, Homing Shot, Missiles)
safety_signals.meaning: enhanced player firepower and tactical options

safety_signals.visual_form: power-up collectibles that increase ship's standard shot power
safety_signals.meaning: incremental damage increase for player weaponry

safety_signals.visual_form: Border item surrounding front of ship

safety_signals.meaning: score accumulation — non-tactical safety but reward signal

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Blaster Fighter star ships (two variants available for player selection)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: player-controlled ships positioned left side of screen during horizontal scroll

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: enemies and gun emplacements emerge from fortress architecture across six stages
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: fortress grows larger/more detailed as stages progress; destination of horizontal scroll

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: mechanical fortress orbiting Earth in opening context
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: static fortress position establishes threat before stage engagement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: parallax scrolling environment with enemy formations, projectiles, and destructible stage elements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: continuous horizontal scroll; enemies spawn and attack; player navigates and responds with equipped weapons

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: six sequential stages with increasing visual detail and architectural complexity of fortress
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemy patterns intensify; fortress sections become visible; final confrontation implied through stage progression

environmental_storytelling.example: six scrolling stages reveal different sections of mechanical fortress, suggesting approach to core structure
enemy ship types and arrangements emerging from fortress suggest organized defensive systems
mechanical aesthetic reinforces 'mysterious fortress' identity through consistent technological imagery
parallax effects in Genesis port create layered foreground/background distinction, adding environmental context

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: firepower and shot pattern changes based on equipped weapon type (Striker adds Options, Homing Shot tracks targets, Six Way Shot fires in multiple directions)
collision immunity against foreground objects while Border item active
attack patterns emerge from fortress architecture; varying projectile trajectories and timing indicate tactical difficulty scaling
uses standard shot pattern until weapon pickup collected

notes: Air Buster is a purely mechanical shmup with minimal narrative signaling. World-state communication relies entirely on visual progression (horizontal scroll toward fortress) and enemy behavior patterns. Faction distinction is binary: player vs. fortress threat. No environmental dialogue or text-based storytelling; all meaning conveyed through entity design and spatial positioning. The 'mysterious mechanical fortress' framing establishes stakes through premise rather than visual detail. Genesis port enhanced visual communication through detailed graphics and parallax depth cues compared to arcade version.
